question.title: Ranking Sales by Region
question.desc: Rank sales representatives in each region based on their total sales. The sales representatives should be ranked in descending order of their sales within each region.
question.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS regions; CREATE TABLE sales (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_id INTEGER, sales_rep TEXT, sales_amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); CREATE TABLE regions (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions (id, region_name) VALUES (1, 'North'), (2, 'South'); INSERT INTO sales (region_id, sales_rep, sales_amount) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', 10000.00), (1, 'Jane Smith', 8000.00), (2, 'Bob Johnson', 12000.00), (2, 'Alice Brown', 9000.00);
question.solution: SELECT r.region_name, s.sales_rep, s.sales_amount, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY r.id ORDER BY s.sales_amount DESC) AS rank FROM sales s JOIN regions r ON s.region_id = r.id ORDER BY r.region_name, s.sales_amount DESC;
question.tips: Using DENSE_RANK() instead of RANK() ensures that if two sales reps have the same sales amount, they will have the same rank and the next rep will have a rank that is one more than the previous rank.
hints: You will need to use a window function to rank the sales reps in each region.
The DENSE_RANK() window function will be useful in this scenario, as it assigns a unique rank to each distinct sales amount within a partition.
The PARTITION BY clause should be used with the region id to ensure that the ranking is done separately for each region.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS regions; CREATE TABLE sales (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_id INTEGER, sales_rep TEXT, sales_amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); CREATE TABLE regions (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions (id, region_name) VALUES (1, 'East'), (2, 'West'); INSERT INTO sales (region_id, sales_rep, sales_amount) VALUES (1, 'Mike Davis', 15000.00), (1, 'Emily Chen', 11000.00), (2, 'David Lee', 13000.00), (2, 'Sophia Patel', 10000.00);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS regions; CREATE TABLE sales (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_id INTEGER, sales_rep TEXT, sales_amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); CREATE TABLE regions (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, region_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions (id, region_name) VALUES (1, 'Central'), (2, 'Coastal'); INSERT INTO sales (region_id, sales_rep, sales_amount) VALUES (1, 'Tom White', 20000.00), (1, 'Kate Martin', 18000.00), (2, 'Peter Hall', 16000.00), (2, 'Linda Brooks', 14000.00);
